title: Ulrich and Helene are Graaff-Reinet Bowling Club's Open Singles winners
bodyText:
[{"type":"paragraph","text":"GRAAFF-REINET NEWS - On 22 and 23 February, spectators were entertained by bowls of a high calibre at the Graaff-Reinet Bowling Club.","html":"GRAAFF-REINET NEWS - On 22 and 23 February, spectators were entertained by bowls of a high calibre at the Graaff-Reinet Bowling Club."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Men","html":"Men"},{"type":"blockquote","text":"14 bowlers took part in the men's competition, with the bowlers competing in three sections, on a round-robin basis."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"After Saturday's play, the section leaders were as follows:","html":"After Saturday's play, the section leaders were as follows:"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Section 1 - Niel Colyn on 3 game points and + 25 shots (after 4 games). Johan Jansen on 3 + 8 still had one game to play on Sunday morning and victory would see him as the section winner.","html":"Section 1 - Niel Colyn on 3 game points and + 25 shots (after 4 games). Johan Jansen on 3 + 8 still had one game to play on Sunday morning and victory would see him as the section winner."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Section 2 - Stefan Ittershagen-Strauss on 2 + 12 (after 2 games), with Wally Koetaan on 2 + 11 (after three games). Ittershagen-Strauss still had two games to play, whereas Koetaan only had one game to play on Sunday morning.Section 3 - Ulrich Steynberg remained unbeaten for the day and claimed the section on 3 + 25.","html":"Section 2 - Stefan Ittershagen-Strauss on 2 + 12 (after 2 games), with Wally Koetaan on 2 + 11 (after three games). Ittershagen-Strauss still had two games to play, whereas Koetaan only had one game to play on Sunday morning.
Section 3 - Ulrich Steynberg remained unbeaten for the day and claimed the section on 3 + 25."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"On Sunday morning, Johan Jansen won his last game in Section 1 to win the section on 4 + 19."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Stefan Ittershagen-Strauss won one of his two games on Sunday to edge out Wally Koetaan as the winner of Section 2 on 3 + 28.","html":"Stefan Ittershagen-Strauss won one of his two games on Sunday to edge out Wally Koetaan as the winner of Section 2 on 3 + 28."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The Men's Open now had three semi-finalists and Martin Verwey filled the fourth spot as the 'best' runner-up in the three sections with a plus factor of 1.66, edging out Colyn and Koetaan.","html":"The Men's Open now had three semi-finalists and Martin Verwey filled the fourth spot as the 'best' runner-up in the three sections with a plus factor of 1.66, edging out Colyn and Koetaan."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Semi-finals","html":"Semi-finals"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Ulrich Steynberg defeated Stefan Ittershagen-Strauss in one of the semi-finals by 21 - 7, while Martin Verwey edged out Johan Jansen by 21 - 19.","html":"Ulrich Steynberg defeated Stefan Ittershagen-Strauss in one of the semi-finals by 21 - 7, while Martin Verwey edged out Johan Jansen by 21 - 19."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Ladies","html":"Ladies"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The ladies competition started out with eight bowlers, playing in two sections on a round-robin basis.","html":"The ladies competition started out with eight bowlers, playing in two sections on a round-robin basis."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Section 1 was reduced to three bowlers as a bowler was forced to withdraw due to health reasons."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"This left Charlene Colyn as the section winner on 2 + 32.","html":"This left Charlene Colyn as the section winner on 2 + 32."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"In Section 2, Helene de Wet remained unbeaten on 3 + 30 and qualified for the final.","html":"In Section 2, Helene de Wet remained unbeaten on 3 + 30 and qualified for the final."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Finals","html":"Finals"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Ulrich Steynberg defeated Martin Verwey by 21 shots to 14 in an entertaining men's final, although the score is no reflection of the quality of bowls played.","html":"Ulrich Steynberg defeated Martin Verwey by 21 shots to 14 in an entertaining men's final, although the score is no reflection of the quality of bowls played."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The ladies' final was a ding-dong battle from the start between Charlene Colyn and Helene de Wet.","html":"The ladies' final was a ding-dong battle from the start between Charlene Colyn and Helene de Wet."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"With the score at 19 - 19, Colyn was holding shot millimetres from the jack.","html":"With the score at 19 - 19, Colyn was holding shot millimetres from the jack."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"De Wet had one bowl remaining and played a 'perfect' shot to edge out Colyn's bowl, pick up two shots and win the title as 2025 Ladies Open Singles Champion.","html":"De Wet had one bowl remaining and played a 'perfect' shot to edge out Colyn's bowl, pick up two shots and win the title as 2025 Ladies Open Singles Champion."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Business League"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The Build it Business League commences on Thursday evening 27 February and the following four Thursdays.","html":"The Build it Business League commences on Thursday evening 27 February and the following four Thursdays."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: Ulrich and Helene are Graaff-Reinet Bowling Club's Open Singles winners
